channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Don Gonyea's front-row seat to presidential history
item.description: As Don Gonyea covered the launch of the Barack Obama Presidential Center in Chicago this week, he recalled key moments from presidential library openings he has covered throughout his career.
item.content:encoded: As Don Gonyea covered the launch of the Barack Obama Presidential Center in Chicago this week, he recalled key moments from presidential library openings he has covered throughout his career.
item.dc:creator: Matt Ozug

item.title: The cruelty behind 'Minnesota nice'
item.description: George Floyd's murder put Minneapolis in the spotlight. Justin Ellis' new book, 'The Cruelty of Nice Folks: Why Minneapolis is the Story of America,' says the city embodies a contradiction - liberal ideals alongside deep racial disparities.
item.content:encoded: George Floyd's murder put Minneapolis in the spotlight. Justin Ellis' new book, 'The Cruelty of Nice Folks: Why Minneapolis is the Story of America,' says the city embodies a contradiction - liberal ideals alongside deep racial disparities.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: The podcast 'When the World Comes to Texas' examines soccer's big moment in a football state
item.description: The hosts of the podcast 'When the World Comes to Texas,' Miranda Suarez and Ron Corning, talk about what they learned about Texas while reporting on the World Cup in their state.
item.content:encoded: The hosts of the podcast 'When the World Comes to Texas,' Miranda Suarez and Ron Corning, talk about what they learned about Texas while reporting on the World Cup in their state.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levitt

item.title: DOJ memo stokes fear among disability advocates of a return to institutionalization
item.description: The Justice Department's opinion challenges civil rights protections that have long treated the institutionalization of disabled Americans as a last resort.
item.content:encoded:
The Justice Department's opinion challenges civil rights protections that have long treated the institutionalization of disabled Americans as a last resort.

(Image credit: Patrick Semansky)
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: Minneapolis high school swimmer invents portable device to measure chloramine levels
item.description: Indoor pools can have a distinct odor caused by chlorine binding to body waste. A Minnesota student invented a device to measure chloramine levels.
item.content:encoded: Indoor pools can have a distinct odor caused by chlorine binding to body waste. A Minnesota student invented a device to measure chloramine levels.
item.dc:creator: Diantha Parker

item.title: Minnesota House Speaker Melissa Hortman and husband honored 1 year after their murders
item.description: It's been a year since a gunman murdered Minnesota state Sen. Melissa Hortman, which is reflected on by another state senator who was also targeted.
item.content:encoded: It's been a year since a gunman murdered Minnesota state Sen. Melissa Hortman, which is reflected on by another state senator who was also targeted.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson

item.title: Palestinian-American kids find inspiration, and escape, on the soccer pitch
item.description: A dozen miles away from the World Cup games in New York/ New Jersey Stadium, Palestinian-American kids turn to soccer as an escape from the realities of war.
item.content:encoded:
A dozen miles away from the World Cup games in New York/ New Jersey Stadium, Palestinian-American kids turn to soccer as an escape from the realities of war.

(Image credit: José A. Alvarado Jr. for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Garsd

item.title: 'Grandmother of Juneteenth' celebrates freedom, 2.5 miles at a time
item.description: Opal Lee is known as the Grandmother of Juneteenth. NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talked with her in 2024 about her activism, and her hopes for the future.
item.content:encoded: Opal Lee is known as the Grandmother of Juneteenth. NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talked with her in 2024 about her activism, and her hopes for the future.
item.dc:creator: Erika Ryan

item.title: Chicago celebrates Obama Presidential Center opening, but community concerns persist
item.description: Some supporters view the Obama Presidential Center as a welcomed investment in a historically underserved area, while long-time residents and community members worry it may accelerate gentrification.
item.content:encoded: Some supporters view the Obama Presidential Center as a welcomed investment in a historically underserved area, while long-time residents and community members worry it may accelerate gentrification.
item.dc:creator: Araceli Gomez-Aldana

item.title: Air Force One, gifted to Trump from Qatar, arrives at Joint Base Andrews
item.description: The luxury Boeing 747, initially valued at $400 million, arrived ahead of schedule on Friday. The jet caused controversy as one of the biggest foreign gifts ever received by the U.S. government.
item.content:encoded:
The luxury Boeing 747, initially valued at $400 million, arrived ahead of schedule on Friday. The jet caused controversy as one of the biggest foreign gifts ever received by the U.S. government.

(Image credit: Alex Wong
)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger
